name: System Boot and Shutdown Events
description: Tracks system start, shutdown, restart, and unexpected shutdown events. Kernel-Power 41 (dirty shutdown) indicates crash, BSOD, or hard power loss — critical for timeline reconstruction. Events 6005/6006 bookend system uptime periods. Event 1074 captures who initiated shutdown/restart and why.
author: EventHawk (SANS/Microsoft/Sigma)

event_descriptions.12: Kernel-General: OS started at time — StartTime field. System start timestamp.
event_descriptions.13: Kernel-General: OS shutdown at time — StopTime field. Clean shutdown timestamp.
event_descriptions.41: Kernel-Power: System rebooted without clean shutdown — BugcheckCode (0=power loss, non-zero=BSOD code). Critical for timeline gaps.
event_descriptions.1074: Initiated restart or shutdown — ProcessName (who), Reason (code), Comment, Type (restart/shutdown/power off)
event_descriptions.1076: Previous unexpected shutdown explanation confirmed — Reason code entered by user
event_descriptions.6005: Event Log service started — system boot marker. Use as start of Windows session.
event_descriptions.6006: Event Log service stopped — clean shutdown marker. Use as end of Windows session.
event_descriptions.6008: Unexpected previous shutdown — PreviousShutdownTime. Previous session ended without 6006 = crash/power loss.
event_descriptions.6009: OS version logged at startup — ProductName, CurrentBuildNumber, CSDVersion, RegisteredOrganization
event_descriptions.6013: System uptime in seconds — useful for computing boot time from event timestamp
